回上一頁	國立金門大學	 
 	教學綱要	 

部別 : 日間部學士班	114學年度第2學期	列印日期 : 2026/05/24
科目名稱 : 專題研究(一) (Directed Research I)	開課班級 : 應英三年級	學　　分 : 2.0	授課時數 : 2.0
授課教師 : 蘇淑品羅主恩	必選修 : 必修	 	 


1.教學目標
Course Objectives: Students will be expected to participate in discussions and respond to readings and listening materials. In addition to the reading materials and exercises in the required textbook, students will study new specific vocabulary, do exercises and tests on the course topics. Activities: various activities assigned, principally those included in the textbook.

6.成績評定及課堂要求
All assignments are due on the due date. Late assignments will NOT be accepted.  
Feel free to email me any time you want, if you have questions or comments. Evaluation: Mid-term examination = 50% Final examination = 50% Participation The instructor reserves the right to deduct points for poor attendance or participation. There may be additional quizzes. If so, the point system will be altered as discussed with students in class. Attendance: One class or 2 hours of absence will be permitted without penalty; 2 points will be subtracted from your FINAL GRADE for every additional hour of absence. If you are more than 10 minutes late, you are counted as absent for that hour. If your unexcused absences are more than 5 days (or 10 hours) you Fail the class. Contacting the Instructor: Students are encouraged to visit the instructor in his office to discuss academic matters during office hours (and during other drop in hours when he is in or by appointment) and by text (0978882975) or email (drjohnrbaker@yahoo.com). If you text or email, please include your name, student number, and class (e.g., conversation), and day and time. Attendance and Punctuality: As attention to attendance and punctuality is expected, these two areas will not be credited. However, inattention to these areas will result in a reduction of the student’s overall grade. Late Assignments: Unexcused late homework will not be graded. Excused late homework will receive a reduced grade. Late assignments will be accepted only with the permission of the instructor. Plagiarism: If a student submits work in whole or part that does not appear to be his/her own (e.g., from a website, too similar to a classmate’s, or markedly different from past performance), the student will be asked to have a teacher-student conference. If the student is found to have indeed committed plagiarism, and I’m sure this will be a rare occurrence, the student will fail the assignment. The student may also fail the course and/or be reported to the English department. Class Participation: All students are expected to come to class having read the group readings, completed required assignments, and prepared to actively participate in in-class activities. Active participation requires that you share your ideas in class and work with students and be respectful of others. Students L1 Usage: Students are encouraged to speak English in the class. Students will be allowed to use their first language when it facilitates our classroom atmosphere (e.g. limited amounts of translation). Personal Electronics and Acceptable Use Policy: Personal electronics (e.g. cell phones, Ipads, etc.) should be turned off and put away during class unless the instructor has specifically given permission for their use for educational purposes. If you do not to follow this policy, your grade will be affected. You may also be counted as absent. Students with Disabilities: If you have a disability and need accommodations to fully participate in this class, please let the instructor know via e-mail during the first week of the course. Please also see also contact Disability Support Services for support. 
 


8.永續發展目標(SDGs)：SDG3 健康與福祉、SDG4 優質教育


9.大學社會責任(USR)關聯性：高
